title: docker-compose: Server did not provide an image ID
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I try to use podman with docker-compose and reuse my old config.

docker-compose with podman works for repository-images but seems to fail with locally-built images.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

enable podman socket at /run/docker.sock

2.

*docker-compose.yml*
``` yml
version: '3.7'
services:
  gitea:
    build: "."
    environment:
      - DB_TYPE=postgres
      - DB_HOST=db:5432
      - DB_NAME=gitea
      - DB_USER=gitea
      - DB_PASSWD=gitea
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - git_data:/data
    ports:
      - 3000:3000
  db:
    image: postgres:alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=gitea
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=gitea
      - POSTGRES_DB=gitea
    restart: always
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    expose:
      - 5432
volumes:
  db_data:
  git_data:
```

*Dockerfile*
```
from gitea/gitea:latest

RUN echo "just to build something"
```


`docker-compose build`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ docker-compose build
db uses an image, skipping
Building gitea
Sending build context to Docker daemon  3.584kB
STEP 1/2: FROM gitea/gitea:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN echo "just to build something"
just to build something
COMMIT docker_gitea
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:b2d5eeeaba3a22b9b8aa97261957974a6bd65274ebd43e1d81d0a7b8b752b116
Copying blob sha256:940aa658ccb5e41d1d0c9694eeb3a4a9a281ac7ce4807a00b123988b0200b4ac
Copying blob sha256:18654675f84f98fc2059ed5abcf44a2f5140ea4de7d6765776a0de37b28be8c2
Copying blob sha256:e2351d9472e9d06e724e6e279fe6c3ff1607d323f09f44c8f184d2831394a1ad
Copying blob sha256:28822be09a0f444d51fc57ed2b4acbc4a63f1ec7798d1adf08b328bf7f1bbde6
Copying blob sha256:0869d4c93150748795550b85a3fba726178cb09a074088fdcf38fda3e51bfd51
Copying blob sha256:97d1c505a1112cf60d4502760043ce0c3762c194716e755f042651d6d90b37ad
Copying blob sha256:13d2687b7946da833edda21b8f7d3968a6579dc103ba80aeff49af34683be162
Copying config sha256:9cf6441c497d0a7fd9d6fe2c21a61f967e08271fcd2c942fe87ab62d4233c355
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 9cf6441c497
Successfully tagged localhost/docker_gitea:latest
9cf6441c497d0a7fd9d6fe2c21a61f967e08271fcd2c942fe87ab62d4233c355
Successfully built 9cf6441c497d
Successfully tagged docker_gitea
Server did not provide an image ID. Cannot write /tmp/tmp1p546fld
ERROR: Service 'gitea' failed to build : Build failed
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

api-compatibility with docker

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.3.0
API Version:  3.3.0

Git Commit:   98f252a3a1a8f1ee00f9f96c6ba00500954b5093-dirty

OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body: No, built images remain local only with docker aswell.

I am talking about how they are named in the container store.

```
$ cat /tmp/test/Dockerfile 
from fedora
run echo hi

$ buildah bud -t test /tmp/test
STEP 1/2: FROM fedora
STEP 2/2: run echo hi
hi
COMMIT test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 0203e243f1ca skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 6526bf8b3fa6 done  
Copying config b477f22c00 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> b477f22c00d
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
b477f22c00d80082424ef5819cca29ac58f6ecd215e189ec80f126f7efb2a510
```
```
$ buildah images test
REPOSITORY       TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
localhost/test   latest   b477f22c00d8   5 seconds ago   184 MB
```
Notice the image is called localhost/test
Now build with Docker
```
$ sudo docker build -t test /tmp/test
Sending build context to Docker daemon  2.048kB
Step 1/2 : from fedora
 ---> 33c4a622f37c
Step 2/2 : run echo hi
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 731059642f1a
Successfully built 731059642f1a
Successfully tagged test:latest
```
Now look at how docker sees the image.
```
$ sudo docker images test
REPOSITORY   TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
test         latest    731059642f1a   13 seconds ago   177MB
```
Docker lists the test without a registry, (Defaulting internally to docker.io)


```

I cannot reproduce this issue. Are you sure docker-compose is pointed to your podman socket? It looks like this is a docker issue https://github.com/docker/cli/issues/2971. Anyway setting `COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=0` as workaround should make it work.
I am quite sure because i tried both and it worked with docker listening on the socket and failed with podman. I will look into it again the next days… thanks!

i can confirm that images are built with different prefix:

### docker:

```
[simon@simonlaptop docker]$ COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=0 && docker-compose build
db uses an image, skipping
Building gitea
Sending build context to Docker daemon  3.584kB
Step 1/2 : from gitea/gitea:latest
 ---> 7737d8e9cae8
Step 2/2 : RUN echo "just to build something"
 ---> Using cache
 ---> 3ce0117563a0
Successfully built 3ce0117563a0
Successfully tagged docker_gitea:latest
```

```
[simon@simonlaptop docker]$ docker image ls
REPOSITORY                                      TAG             IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
docker_gitea                                    latest          3ce0117563a0   16 seconds ago   148MB
postgres                                        alpine          b8c450ae0903   3 days ago       192MB
gitea/gitea                                     latest          7737d8e9cae8   9 days ago       148MB
```

### podman:

```
[simon@simonlaptop docker]$ COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=0 && docker-compose build

Copying blob sha256:940aa658ccb5e41d1d0c9694eeb3a4a9a281ac7ce4807a00b123988b0200b4ac

Server did not provide an image ID. Cannot write /tmp/tmps0rm328_

```

```
[simon@simonlaptop docker]$ docker image ls
REPOSITORY                                      TAG             IMAGE ID       CREATED         SIZE
gitea/gitea                                     latest          7737d8e9cae8   9 days ago      149MB
postgres                                        alpine          16a925851698   2 weeks ago     195MB
localhost/docker_gitea                          latest          9d33c7dc9282   5 days ago      149MB
```

however, starting the thing with `docker-compose up` works with podman after the build.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I believe this issue is fixed, reopen if I am mistaken.

This persists on Fedora 35 when using the Podman user socket through `DOCKER_HOST` and Docker Compose with i.e. https://gitlab.com/omd_public/assemblage.

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.4.7
API Version:  3.4.7
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Mon Jul 11 00:35:25 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

I cannot reopen this issue. Should I recreate it?

Please try a more recent Podman. We just released 4.2 last week.
